title: localization — model relationships
description: PlatformLanguage is the aggregate root for the locale catalogue. It references geography for ISO-639 + ISO-3166 truth (replacing the previous field duplication). TenantLocale + Translation FK to PlatformLanguage via `language_id` (concept-name column; target table is `platform_languages`).

internal.description: Language enablement per tenant. Refuses hard delete while any TenantLocale references this row.
PlatformLanguages cannot be deleted while any tenant has them enabled.
The job that produced each row (null when hand-authored or file-imported).

outbound.description: Wave 5 addition. Source of truth for ISO-639-1 code, English name, native name, direction / is_rtl. Every PlatformLanguage row MUST reference a canonical geography::Language row — the FK is NOT NULL. Restrict on delete so vendor row purges can't orphan our platform-language rows.

Wave 5 addition. Populated ONLY for regional variants (`fr-CA` → Canada, `en-GB` → United Kingdom, `pt-BR` → Brazil). NULL for base languages that span many countries (English, French, Spanish base rows). Source of truth for flag_emoji + native country name for the variant.
Null = platform-level default translation (Stackra-shipped). Non-null = tenant override.

inbound.description: Domain models with translated columns compose HasTranslations. No FK inbound — they store translations in their own JSONB `translations` column rather than joining to our Translation table (that's for translation keys + Laravel-style i18n, not per-row content).

accessor_chain.description: Callers rarely touch geography directly — the PlatformLanguage model surfaces the joined fields via accessors so the API keeps the same shape it had before the refactor.
